key_insight: GutCore is a foundation model that can analyze an entire endoscopy exam, not just single frames, to estimate gastric cancer presence, invasion depth, biomarkers, and prognosis at the patient level.
clinical_relevance: If validated externally, this approach could help standardize whole-exam endoscopic interpretation and support earlier, more precise gastric cancer risk stratification.
method: Pretrained on 5.6 million de-identified endoscopic images from more than 10 hospitals.
Evaluated on open image-level datasets and an internal cohort of 11,035 endoscopic examinations.
Aggregated all images from each exam to predict cancer status, invasion depth, biomarker status, and overall survival.
evidence_signal: In the internal cohort, GutCore achieved AUC 0.995 for cancer detection, 0.960 for muscularis propria invasion, and 0.804 for SM2-or-deeper invasion, with strong survival separation in advanced gastric cancer.
watch_item: The findings come from retrospective, de-identified data and still require independent external validation before routine clinical use.
paper_title: GutCore: An Endoscopy Foundation Model for Whole-Case Gastric Cancer Analysis
